name: arXiv Preprint
notes: arXiv preprint format. No page limit. No blind review. Include all supplementary material inline. Longer abstract OK (up to 500 words). Can be more detailed than camera-ready versions. Include appendices freely.
writing_guide:
TONE: Thorough and detailed. Active voice preferred. You have space, so be comprehensive, but do not be verbose. Every paragraph should add information, not just words. Write for specialists in your subfield who want full technical details.

ABSTRACT: 300-500 words. More detailed than journal versions. Include: problem context, specific contributions (numbered list OK), methodology summary, quantitative results, and implications. Mention if code/data is available.

STRUCTURE: Standard IMRAD but expanded. Include everything reviewers at a target venue would want to see, plus material that would normally go in supplementary. Full experimental details, hyperparameter tables, additional ablations, extended related work. Use appendices freely for proofs, additional results, dataset details. Consider a 'Reproducibility' section with code links, hardware specs, and training times.

CITATIONS: 40-80+ references. Be thorough in Related Work; this is your chance to be comprehensive since there is no page limit. Discuss how your work relates to each cited work, not just list them. Use \citet and \citep.

FIGURES/TABLES: 8-15+ display items. Include everything useful: training curves, ablation tables, qualitative examples, failure cases, computational cost comparisons. No limit, but every figure must add value.

REVIEWER EXPECTATIONS: Preprint readers expect completeness. Include negative results and failure modes. Provide enough detail for full reproduction. Version your paper clearly (v1, v2) with revision notes if updating.

DO NOT: Be sloppy because it is 'just a preprint'; arXiv papers are widely read and cited. Do not omit experimental details you would include in supplementary material. Do not forget to include a link to code/data if available.
